text.title: activation-framework Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for activation-framework. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Define activation as the specific behavior that correlates most strongly with D30+ retention — not as completing onboarding or creating an account.
rule.rationale: Internal milestones ("completed setup wizard") are easy to optimize but predict nothing. Retention-correlated behaviors ("shared a doc within 48h", "ran 2 reports within 7d") are the real signal.

rule.statement: Calculate baseline activation rate and time-to-activation before making any changes; segment by acquisition channel to identify if the problem is channel-specific.
rule.rationale: Without a baseline, you cannot measure improvement. Channel segmentation often reveals that the problem is concentrated in one source (e.g. paid ads have 15% activation vs organic at 45%).

rule.statement: Watch at least 20 session recordings before forming hypotheses; agents cannot replace qualitative pattern recognition from real user behavior.
rule.rationale: Funnel data shows where users drop but not why. Session recordings reveal whether the cause is confusion, missing information, performance issues, or unclear next steps.

rule.statement: Score every experiment hypothesis on Impact (1-10), Confidence (1-10), and Ease (1-10); prioritize by ICE average; run highest-scoring experiment first.
rule.rationale: ICE forces explicit judgment about ROI before investing engineering effort. Without scoring, teams default to easiest changes (high Ease, low Impact) rather than highest-value work.
